title: Fedora 31: Can't run selenium/hub container image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Can't run selenium/hub:3 image and get the following error:
```
âžś podman logs hub
2020-01-19 07:34:37,788 INFO Included extra file "/etc/supervisor/conf.d/selenium-hub.conf" during parsing
2020-01-19 07:34:37,790 INFO supervisord started with pid 3
2020-01-19 07:34:38,792 INFO spawnerr: unknown error making dispatchers for 'selenium-hub': EACCES
2020-01-19 07:34:39,793 INFO gave up: selenium-hub entered FATAL state, too many start retries too quickly
```
And after I delete `/var/home/xiaofwan/.config/containers/libpod.conf`, `libpod.conf` can't be generated automatically

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run `podman run -d --network host --name hub --privileged selenium/hub:3`

**Describe the results you received:**
Can't get container started

**Describe the results you expected:**
Have container started without any error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.7.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  IDMappings:

      host_id: 100000

  MemFree: 5421518848
  MemTotal: 8215134208

    name: crun

    version: |-
      crun version 0.10.6
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 8359243776

    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  uptime: 2h 37m 9.04s (Approximately 0.08 days)
```

```
podman-1.7.0-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I take it, you are running rootless?  Can you try running this container as root.
My usual suspects on this type of issue, is something in User Namespace is blocking the access.  Of course another potential problem would be SELinux.  So if SELinux is enabled, could you try with `setenforce 0`

Thanks @rhatdan! You always have such a quick response! :+1: 
1. Yes, I'm running rootless.
2. Tried run with root, same error log.
3. Still have same issue after setting SELinux to Permissive mode.
4. Podman is able to create libpod.conf automatically if it does not exist. But it does not work in Silverblue. I tried on my Fedora 31 machine, it works. Is it related with home directory different? (`/var/home/xiaofwan` for Silverblue, not `/home/xiaofwan`)
Well if this fails as root, then it is not a rootless issue.  Does the container work with docker?  Does the container work if you run it with `--privileged` flag?

This image is download from `docker.io/selenium/hub`, and same image works on my Fedora 31 machine running with `podman`.
No, it does not work with `--privileged` flag.
@rhatdan, I can use the `--user root` option to get the hub and nodes running. Do you have any ideas about the reason? Thanks!

You are saying you needed to run this command
```
$ podman run --user root -d --network host --name hub --privileged selenium/hub:3
```
And it worked?  This should be the default, unless selenium/hub:3 defaults to running as a non root user?
Yes. It worked. The `selenium/hub:3` runs as `seluser`, not `root`.
Then that means the selenium/hub image is incorrect and should be fixed.  It seems like there is a permission issue, and the seluser is not able to do what it needs.
Got it! Thanks @rhatdan.

@rhatdan, I tried same container image `selenium/hub:3` on Fedora 30, everything works well.
The difference is `cgrouip v1` vs `cgroup v2` and `runc` vs `crun`. Is that possible that the cgroup or crun caused this issue? Thanks!

BTW: This issue is not related with Silverblue. My Fedora 31 machine has the same issue.

**Info on Fedora 30:**
`podman version`
```
Version:            1.7.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.14
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
`podman info --debug`
```
debug:       
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""       
  go version: go1.12.14   
  podman version: 1.7.0        
host:      
  BuildahVersion: 1.12.0
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:  
    package: conmon-2.0.2-1.fc30.x86_64

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.2, commit: a89d21975ee86e84e0b0e1c0f887687582f4b0e3'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora      
    version: "30"      
  MemFree: 169222144
  MemTotal: 1024778240                    
  OCIRuntime:    
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-95.rc9.gitc1485a1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-                        
      runc version 1.0.0-rc9+dev        
      commit: 0840802d505e28d3adcbc7a275cbeadbdf64ddec
      spec: 1.0.1-dev      
  SwapFree: 1291567104          
  SwapTotal: 1291841536    
  arch: amd64             
  cpus: 1    
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: m1.cockpit.lan            
  kernel: 5.4.7-100.fc30.x86_64                                                                
  os: linux
  rootless: false                              
  uptime: 7m 43.26s
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

Hey @rhatdan,
I downgrade `podman` to `1.6.2` from `1.7.0` on Fedora 31. Running `podman run -d --network host --name hub --privileged selenium/hub:latest` (non-root) works.
Any changes between 1.6.2 and 1.7.0 caused this issue? Thanks!
If you only downgraded Podman, it's not `crun` or CGroups v2
Can you confirm that going back to podman 1.7.0, it is broken again?

I cannot reproduce on F31 in the very same setup (crun, cgroupsv2, podman 1.7).

@henrywang, are you still seeing the issue?

@henrywang Earlier you said it was working on NON Silverblue F31, now it is broken?  Have you tried to identify exactly what is failing within the container?

You also said that if you launch this with --user root it works, is this still the case?

Thanks @rhatdan.
1. Yes, it was working on F31(non-silverblue) before (with podman `1.6.2`), and it's broken now because my F31(non-silverblue) got system update (podman updated to `1.7.0` and then `1.8.0`).
2. Looks `supervisord` cannot start selenium service
3. `--user root` works on podman `1.8.0` and `1.7.0`

Summary: (Same selenium/hub:3 image)
1. Podman `1.6.2` works even no `--user root` added.
2. Podman `1.7.0` and `1.8.0` work only with `--user root` added.

Have you been able to strace what is blowing up?

Most likely would be some file/directory path has changed that used to be accessible by non-root is now changed.  And the processes inside the container are getting permission denied. 

I would figure it is something in /dev or one of the mounted file system objects.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this has been over a month with no response, I will close.  Reopen if this is still happening and you have more output.
